message:
fix(markdown): Correct nested list indentation calculation

Fixes list item indentation to properly reflect nesting depth.

The previous calculation used `saturating_sub(1)` on list_stack.len(),
which caused the first level of nesting to have zero indent. This made
nested lists appear flush with their parent items.

The fix removes the subtraction so indent is calculated directly from
list_stack.len(), ensuring:
- Top-level items get proper base indentation
- Each nesting level adds the configured list_indent spacing
- Visual hierarchy correctly represents list structure

Before: Nested items appeared at same indent as parent
After:  Each nesting level indents by list_indent spaces

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
